channel.title: Eternal Record
channel.description: Daily cinematic mythology stories — 90-second vertical videos.
channel.itunes:author: Ayush Rana

item.title: The Pride of Arjuna
item.description: You think you know this story. You don't. In the great war of Kurukshetra, Arjuna stood at the forefront, his chariot wheels turning as he faced the enemy lines. He had been trained by the greatest warriors of his time and had mastered the art of warfare. Duryodhana, the prince of the Kauravas, had provoked Arjuna into this battle, and the very fate of the Pandavas hung in the balance. As the war raged on, Arjuna found himself at the center of it all, striking down enemy after enemy with his unmatched prowess. His brothers, the Pandavas, looked up to him with admiration and awe. Their people cheered his name, calling him the greatest warrior of all time. But as the days passed, Arjuna began to feel a creeping sense of pride. He started to believe that his own greatness was the reason for the Pandavas' victory. He saw himself as invincible, and his brothers as mere mortals who couldn't match his skill. This is Pride — the delusion that you are invincible. The moment Arjuna realized his pride had consumed him, the tide of the war began to turn. His brothers fought with renewed strength, and the Pandavas emerged victorious. But the lesson remained — the moment we let pride take hold, we lose sight of what truly matters. Ask yourself, are you letting pride cloud your judgment?
